svg.title: Queens difficulty model
svg.desc: Ten solving tricks with example boards and weights, plus the no-pencil scoring formula and tier table.
svg.text: Solving tricks, their weights, and how a level is scored (no pencil = guessing is the wall)

g.text: One open cell left in a region -
A row or column with one cell
left. No colour cue - easy to
A placed queen blocks its row,
The game marks these for you.
A whole row/column is one colour
A region's cells all sit in one
row -> no other region can use
Queen is within a set of cells
k rows/columns hold only k colours
Assume a queen and follow it.
If a region ends fully blocked
k rows confined to the same k
chain = forced moves + harder tricks (in your head)

svg.text: GuessBase 3 · Mult 10 · then + fatigue round(3·lnN), N = hard moves + guesses
